checks.name: Shewchuk arena: x* == (2,-2) exact and eig(A) == {2, 7}
2x2 exact Fractions: sequential v_i/xi_i == batch conditioning after 1 and 2 obs (Sigma_0 = I)
2x2 exact Fractions: sequential v_i/xi_i == batch conditioning after 1 and 2 obs (Sigma_0 = A^{-1})
chain: sequential == batch conditioning after 4 and 8 random actions (Sigma_0 = I, rel)
chain: sequential == batch conditioning after 4 and 8 random actions (Sigma_0 = A^{-1}, rel)
Gram-Schmidt proposition on 2x2, Sigma_0 = I: <v_i,v_j>_Sigma0 == delta_ij, Sigma_i == Sigma_0 - VV^T, mu_i == V xi
Gram-Schmidt proposition on 2x2, Sigma_0 = A^{-1}: <v_i,v_j>_Sigma0 == delta_ij, Sigma_i == Sigma_0 - VV^T, mu_i == V xi
Gram-Schmidt proposition on chain, Sigma_0 = I: <v_i,v_j>_Sigma0 == delta_ij, Sigma_i == Sigma_0 - VV^T, mu_i == V xi
Gram-Schmidt proposition on chain, Sigma_0 = A^{-1}: <v_i,v_j>_Sigma0 == delta_ij, Sigma_i == Sigma_0 - VV^T, mu_i == V xi
HEADLINE: greedy actions s_n = r_n-1 + prior A^-1 ==> mu_n == pcg iterate x_n, n = 0..32 (chain, rel)
HEADLINE: v_n == p_n / sqrt(p_n^T A p_n) -- the A-normalized CG search directions, all n
HEADLINE: conjugacy p_i^T A p_j = 0  ==  Mahalanobis <v_i,v_j>_Sigma0 = delta_ij (same numbers, two vocabularies)
2x2 exact probabilistic: mu_1 == x_1, mu_2 == x* == (2,-2), Sigma_2 == 0, and Sigma_(n-1) y_n parallel to p_n at both steps (exact Fractions)
2x2 exact: tr(A Sigma_n) == 2, 1, 0 for n = 0, 1, 2 (one unit per observation)
tr(A Sigma_n) == 32 - n for every n (greedy): one unit of A-weighted uncertainty removed per observation
E ||x - mu_n||_A^2 == tr(A Sigma_n) over the prior (K = 2000 draws, fixed actions, n = 0..5, rel tol 5%)
S_n^T (A mu_n - b) == 0 for all n, all three policies (explored subspace residual-free, Wenger et al. 2022)
actual CG A-norm error decreases monotonically (recorded against the posterior band sqrt(tr(A Sigma_n)) -- the band is the PRIOR-average, not per-b)
Corner Case 1: coordinate actions e_1..e_32 + prior A^-1 ==> R_ij = v_i^T y_j is upper triangular and R^T == chol(A) (signs included), R^T R == A
Corner Case 1: V is upper triangular (Gram-Schmidt of e_1..e_n in the A-inner product stays in span{e_1..e_n}) and A^-1 == V V^T
policy race: tr(A Sigma_k) == 32 - k for greedy, coordinate AND random -- identical uncertainty budget, 1 unit per step regardless of policy
policy race: the A-norm ERRORS differ wildly: k = 8 greedy 1.425e+00 vs coordinate 4.392e+00 / random 4.919e+00 (>= 3x); k = 16 greedy 3.533e-01 vs 3.007e+00 / 4.697e+00 (>= 8x)
policy race: all three policies collapse to x* at k = 32 (full rank => posterior is a point) -- same destination, different journeys
2x2 prior ellipse: eigvals of A^-1 == {1/7, 1/2} with axes (1,2), (2,-1) (1-sigma semi-axes 0.37796, 0.70711)
2x2 after obs 1: det Sigma_1 == 0 and y_1^T Sigma_1 y_1 == 0 exactly -- the credible ellipse collapses to a segment (variance along the measured direction is spent)
2x2 after obs 1: x* - mu_1 == (6/83)(22,-5) -- the true solution lies EXACTLY on the collapsed segment line
2x2 steepest descent from x0 = 0: successive residuals orthogonal (zigzag), constant contraction ||e||_A ratio, still 8 steps short of x* while CG is EXACT in 2
calibration: rank(Sigma_5) == 27 and Mahalanobis stat ~ chi^2_27: empirical mean 26.82 vs 27 (rel tol 10%), 90% coverage 0.900 (tol +/-0.07)

notation.action_observation: bbar_i = s_i^T b = y_i^T x,  y_i = A^T s_i
notation.source: Hennig, Pfoertner, Weiland, Probabilistic Numerics tutorial, ICML Seoul 06/07/2026, pp. 7-20; Shewchuk 1994 SS3/SS6/SS7-8

uncertainty.honest_note: tr(A Sigma_n) = E||x-mu_n||_A^2 averages over the PRIOR; the actual error for this particular b is one draw and sits below the band once the greedy policy locks onto it

policy_race_summary.teaching_point: identical tr(A Sigma_k) = 32-k budgets; greedy spends its budget where THIS b lives
